steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find the third side and confirm the obtuse condition
steps.content:
The perimeter equals the sum of all three sides:
$$5 + 9 + x = 27$$
$$x = 13$$
Since the triangle is obtuse, verify using $a^2 + b^2 \lt c^2$ where $c$ is the longest side. With sides 5, 9, and 13:
$$5^2 + 9^2 = 25 + 81 = 106$$
$$13^2 = 169$$
Since $106 \lt 169$, the obtuse condition holds. The third side is 13 meters. That confirms A is correct.
